provenance.source_corpus: Restoration of Zooplankton Communities in Industrially Damaged Lakes: Influences of Residual Metal Contamination and the Recovery of Fish Communities

source_spans.text: Continuing metal toxicity appears to be the primary cause of the absence of some zooplankton species, particularly Daphnia spp. from metal-contaminated lakes.
Continuing metal toxicity appears to be the primary cause of the absence of some zooplankton species, particularly Daphnia spp. from metal-contaminated lakes.
Examination of the data indicated that the species richness of crustacean zooplankton communities increased with decreased metal concentrations.

nodes.name: species richness of crustacean zooplankton communities

source_spans.text: Examination of the data indicated that the species richness of crustacean zooplankton communities increased with decreased metal concentrations.
The abundance of large Daphnia species is usually low in the presence of planktivorous fish.
The abundance of large Daphnia species is usually low in the presence of planktivorous fish.
Fish predation forces zooplankton communities towards smaller-bodied species by selectively removing the larger-bodied forms.
Fish predation forces zooplankton communities towards smaller-bodied species by selectively removing the larger-bodied forms.
Stocking of piscivorous fish species may be needed to control planktivores and aid the reestablishment of typical zooplankton communities.
Stocking of piscivorous fish species may be needed to control planktivores and aid the reestablishment of typical zooplankton communities.
When populations of Daphnia spp. are first becoming established, the abundant perch characteristic of early fish population recovery can be a barrier to successful Daphnia colonization.
When populations of Daphnia spp. are first becoming established, the abundant perch characteristic of early fish population recovery can be a barrier to successful Daphnia colonization.
